content:
Key Findings

  • Fylde Borough Council recorded £157.4M in total spending across 37,514 transactions
  • All £157.4M of spending was categorised as 'Unknown' payment type
  • The council's spending data covers 11 financial years from 2015/16 to 2025/26

Understanding Fylde's Payment Types

Fylde Borough Council's spending data reveals that all £157.4M of its recorded transactions fall under an 'Unknown' payment type category. This lack of specific categorisation makes it challenging to analyse how the council distributes its funds across different payment methods or services. The absence of detailed payment type information limits transparency and makes it difficult for residents to understand exactly where their council tax is being spent.

The council's spending data covers an extensive period from 2015/16 to 2025/26, encompassing 37,514 individual transactions. Despite this comprehensive timeframe, the uniformity of the 'Unknown' categorisation suggests a potential gap in the council's data recording or publishing practices. This uniformity raises questions about the granularity and specificity of the financial information available to the public.

Implications of Unknown Payment Types

The classification of all £157.4M of spending as 'Unknown' has significant implications for financial transparency and public scrutiny. Without clear categorisation, it is difficult for residents and watchdog organisations to assess whether the council's spending aligns with its stated priorities and budgets. This lack of detail can also hinder efforts to identify areas of potential inefficiency or overspending.

For example, understanding the distribution of payments across different services such as waste management, social care, or infrastructure projects is crucial for holding the council accountable. The absence of this information means that residents cannot easily evaluate the effectiveness of their local government's financial management. This gap in transparency could also make it more challenging for the council to demonstrate its compliance with financial regulations and best practices.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Fylde Borough Council about the categorisation of its payment types and methods
  • Attend council committee meetings to ask questions about the transparency of financial data and payment categorisation
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see if more detailed information is available there
  • Write to your local councillor to express concerns about the lack of detailed payment type information and request improvements
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and identify areas for further investigation
This article is based on Fylde Borough Council's published spending data covering 2015/16 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
